team_context.cap_situation: healthy — ~$18-20M in top-51 space after re-signings, with room to create more via restructures. Cleanest cap books in years with Russell Wilson finally off the books. Bo Nix's rookie deal provides massive surplus value through 2027.
team_context.trade_posture: Run-it-back mentality after AFC Championship loss. Re-signed nearly all key free agents. Buyers at WR, TE, LB depth. Willing to entertain offers on select veterans (Stidham, possibly Riley Moss) but not actively selling. Hold 30th overall pick in 2026 draft.

players.name: Bo Nix
players.performance_evidence: 3,931 passing yards, 25 TDs, 11 INTs in 2025 (17 games). 356 rushing yards, 5 rushing TDs. 63.4% completion rate. PFF grade 77.1 (13th among QBs). Led Broncos to 14-3 record and AFC Championship. 24-10 regular season record in 2 NFL seasons. QB7 in fantasy both years. OTC valuation of $35-37M vs $4.7M AAV — enormous surplus value.

trade_value.reasoning: Young franchise QB on a rookie deal with 2 years + 5th-year option + franchise tags of control remaining. The single most valuable asset type in the NFL. Worth multiple first-round picks in practice, but expressed as pick 1 for the scale. No team trades this.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise quarterback entering Year 3 on a bargain rookie deal. Led team to AFC Championship. Denver's entire competitive window is built around Nix's cost-controlled years. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Suffered broken ankle late in Divisional Round win vs Bills (Jan 2026). Had surgery, expected to be fully ready for OTAs/training camp. ESPN's Schefter reported he's expected to be ready for OTAs.

players.name: Jarrett Stidham
players.performance_evidence: Started AFC Championship after Nix's injury: 17-of-31, 133 yards, 1 TD, 1 INT, 1 fumble in 10-7 loss to Patriots. Payton has said he could be a starter for several teams. Reliable veteran backup in Sean Payton's system for 3 years.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on a 1-year, $8M cap hit with active trade interest from multiple teams (Jets, others). Weak QB draft class inflates his value. Worth a 4th-round pick, potentially a 3rd if bidding war develops. Broncos want a 3rd to start conversations.

players.availability_reasoning: Multiple teams have called about Stidham per Dianna Russini of The Athletic. Jets are a known suitor. Broncos would save $6.5M in cap by trading him. However, Payton values him highly and already re-signed Sam Ehlinger as QB3 — they'd need to feel comfortable with backup depth. Price starts at a 3rd-round pick per SI's Kelberman.
players.trade_buzz: Active. Per The Athletic's Russini, multiple teams have inquired. Jets connection via former Broncos exec Darren Mougey. Trade was discussed at 2024 deadline as well.

players.name: J.K. Dobbins
players.performance_evidence: 772 rushing yards in 10 games in 2025 before foot injury (5.0 YPC). 21 runs of 10+ yards led team. Career 5.2 YPC average. Led Broncos in carries and rushing yards. Productive when healthy but significant injury history — missed 27 games over last 3 seasons.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive RB when healthy on a fair 2-year deal, but extensive injury history and low positional value cap his trade value. Worth a mid-to-late 4th rounder. The $6M cap hit is reasonable but RB is a fungible position.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a 2-year deal, Payton loves him, and he's the lead back. Broncos wouldn't move him unless they got a surprisingly strong offer, since RJ Harvey alone isn't proven as a bell cow.
players.injury_notes: Foot injury ended 2025 season after Week 10. History of ACL tear (2021), Achilles tear (2023). Has missed 27 games over last 3 seasons. Has never played a full 17-game season.

players.name: RJ Harvey
players.performance_evidence: 12 touchdowns as a rookie in 2025. Showed ability as receiver and in space. Stepped up when Dobbins went down but snap share was inconsistent early (40% max pre-Dobbins injury). 14 runs of 10+ yards. High upside but unproven as lead back.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap, ascending RB on a rookie deal with 3 years of control. 12 TDs as a rookie show scoring ability. Worth an early 3rd to a RB-needy team, but Denver views him as a core piece of their backfield.

players.availability_reasoning: Denver invested a 2nd-round pick in Harvey and he delivered 12 TDs as a rookie. He's the future at the position even with Dobbins back. Would take a significant overpay to pry him away.

players.name: Courtland Sutton
players.performance_evidence: 2025 Pro Bowl selection. 1,017 receiving yards in 2025 (led team). Had career-high 81 catches in 2024 for 1,081 yards and 8 TDs. Bo Nix's top target and security blanket. Has led Broncos in receiving 4 of last 7 seasons. Signed 4-year, $92M extension in July 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl production on a long-term deal, but age (31) and massive dead cap ($28M) make a trade virtually impossible before 2027. If tradeable, he'd be worth a late 1st/early 2nd given 3+ years of control at a fair AAV.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 4-year, $92M extension in July 2025. $28M dead cap makes a trade financially impossible. He's the WR1 and Nix's top target. Broncos insider Mike Klis says 2026 is not a trade year — the earliest possible off-ramp is 2027.
players.injury_notes: ACL tear in 2020 caused him to miss entire season. Has been healthy and durable since, playing 17 games in each of last 2 seasons.

players.name: Troy Franklin
players.performance_evidence: Showed flashes in Year 2, including a 23-yard TD in the playoff win vs Packers. Young 4th-round pick developing in Payton's system. Beat reporters describe the team as very fond of his trajectory. Still ascending.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap, developing WR on a rookie deal with upside but hasn't yet proven to be a consistent contributor. Worth a mid-4th based on age and potential.

players.availability_reasoning: Broncos are investing in Franklin's development as their WR2 of the future. With the WR room thin behind Sutton, Denver needs him. Only a strong offer would move the needle.

players.name: Pat Bryant
players.performance_evidence: 2025 3rd-round pick from Illinois. Competed with Devaughn Vele for snaps; Vele was traded to New Orleans for a 4th-round pick, clearing the path for Bryant. Young player still developing.

trade_value.reasoning: 3rd-round rookie on a cheap deal with 3 years of control but minimal proven production. Worth a 5th-round pick at best. Denver is thin at WR and unlikely to move him.

players.availability_reasoning: With Vele traded and the WR room thin, Bryant is needed for depth. Broncos invested a 3rd-round pick in him. Not being shopped.

players.name: Evan Engram
players.performance_evidence: 461 receiving yards and 1 TD in 2025 — well below expectations for a 2x Pro Bowler signed to a $23M deal. Described as underwhelming by Denver Post. Bottom-five blocking TE per PFF. Age 32 entering final year of contract. Was a cap casualty candidate per multiple outlets.

trade_value.reasoning: Declining production, expensive final year ($12.5M cap hit), and age (32) severely limit trade value. Would likely need to include salary offset. Worth a late-round pick at most, and even that may be generous.

players.availability_reasoning: Was floated as a cap casualty by multiple outlets (SI, PFR). Engram underwhelmed in 2025 and is entering the final year of his deal at 32. Payton wants to add another receiving TE. Broncos would likely accept a 6th-round pick and salary dump.
players.trade_buzz: PFR reported Engram is not a lock to be released but Payton wants another receiving TE. His 65.7% negative fate probability per OTC's cap casualty model.

players.name: Adam Trautman
players.performance_evidence: Trusted Payton guy since New Orleans days. 55 receptions for 587 yards and 6 TDs over 51 games in Denver. Primarily a blocker, though blocking grades have been poor per PFF. Re-signed to 3-year, $18M deal in March 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE with limited receiving production on a modest deal. Some value as a system fit for Payton-style offenses. Worth a 6th-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed, but Broncos are actively seeking TE upgrades. If they draft or sign a TE1, Trautman becomes expendable depth at a modest cap hit.

players.name: Garett Bolles
players.performance_evidence: First-team All-Pro in 2025. Led all NFL tackles with 90.7 PFF pass blocking grade. First career Pro Bowl selection in 2025. Started all 17 games. Has been one of the best LTs in football the last 3+ seasons. Signed 4-year, $82M extension in December 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite production at premium OT position, but age (34) and massive dead cap ($30M) make a trade impossible. If hypothetically tradeable, the All-Pro level play and 3 years of control would command a 1st-rounder.

players.availability_reasoning: First-team All-Pro in 2025, just signed a 4-year extension. $30M+ dead cap makes trade financially impossible. Anchor of PFF's #1 ranked O-line.
players.injury_notes: Broke leg in 2022, missed significant time. Has been healthy and started all 17 games in 2024 and 2025.

players.name: Ben Powers
players.performance_evidence: Led all interior OL in run block win rate in 2024. Started all 17 games in 2023 and 2024. Missed 9 games in 2025 with torn biceps but returned for playoffs. Quality guard when healthy. Part of PFF's #1 ranked O-line.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality guard but on an expiring deal with an $18.2M cap hit and $9.8M dead cap — trade economics are poor. Acquiring team gets 1 year at a premium price. Worth a 5th-round pick at best given the contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Was a trade candidate for weeks (ESPN's Fowler named him specifically). However, 9NEWS' Klis reported the Broncos decided NOT to trade, release, or ask for a pay cut. He'll start in 2026 on his current deal. But if a team offered a mid-round pick, Denver might reconsider given Palczewski's emergence.
players.injury_notes: Torn biceps cost him 9 games in 2025. Returned for playoffs.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN's Fowler reported Powers could 'potentially be included in trades.' Broncos subsequently decided to keep him per Klis. Quiet for now, but deal is expiring and situation could change during draft.

players.name: Luke Wattenberg
players.performance_evidence: Extended in-season (November 2025) on a 4-year, $48M deal. Took a notable step forward in 2024 per PFF. Started all 17 games in 2024 and 2025. Part of PFF's #1 ranked O-line.

trade_value.reasoning: Young center on a fair long-term deal with 3 years of control. Solid production on PFF's #1 O-line. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd, but dead cap ($18M) makes a trade highly unlikely.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended in November 2025. $18M dead cap. Only 26 years old. Core piece of the O-line for years to come.

players.name: Quinn Meinerz
players.performance_evidence: 2x First-team All-Pro (2024, 2025). Pro Bowl in 2025. Started all 17 games for 3 consecutive seasons. Played 97% of offensive snaps (1,108 total) in 2025. Arguably the best RG in the NFL. Contract just restructured to create $11M in cap space.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite guard, 27 years old, 3 years of control on a deal that's now looking team-friendly after restructure. 2x First-team All-Pro. Would command multiple high picks in theory but $30M dead cap makes trade impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: $30M dead cap after restructure. 2x First-team All-Pro at age 27. Cornerstone of PFF's #1 ranked O-line. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury briefly interrupted early career. Has not missed a game since Week 6 of 2022 — started all 51 regular season games from 2023-2025.

players.name: Mike McGlinchey
players.performance_evidence: Played arguably his best football in 2025 per Denver Post. Started all 17 games. Highest cap hit on the team at ~$24M. Has stabilized Denver's RT spot. Part of PFF's #1 ranked O-line. Strong locker room leader.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid RT but massively overpaid at $23.8M cap hit. Dead cap of $15.3M makes a pre-June trade a net negative. After June 1, savings improve. Worth a 4th-round pick if salary can be absorbed, but economics are bad for both sides.

players.availability_reasoning: Team-high $23.8M cap hit with Palczewski extended as potential successor. Denver Post says Broncos won't cut him and will let him play out 2026, but a short extension to lower the cap hit has been floated. If a team offered a mid-round pick and could absorb the salary, Denver might listen.
players.trade_buzz: Was mentioned as restructure candidate by multiple outlets. Not actively shopped but cap hit draws attention.

players.name: Zach Allen
players.performance_evidence: First-team All-Pro in 2025. Led NFL in QB hits over past 2 seasons. Signed 4-year, $102M extension in August 2025. 2024 DPOY finalist (per Broncos.com). 14-win defense's best lineman. Plays 3-4 DE but rushes the passer at an elite level.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite defender at age 28 on a 4-year deal with massive guaranteed money. First-team All-Pro. In theory worth multiple 1sts, but $48.7M dead cap makes trade literally impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: $48.7M dead cap — the highest on the roster. Just signed a $102M extension. First-team All-Pro. Cannot be traded under any circumstances.
players.injury_notes: Missed a few games in 2025 with a pectoral injury.

players.name: D.J. Jones
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed in March 2025 to a deal worth ~$13M/year. ESPN's Walder called the Jones re-signing his favorite Broncos move. Solid run-stuffing DT in Vance Joseph's defense. Part of a defensive front that produced 63 sacks (franchise record) in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starting DT on a reasonable 2-year deal. Run-stuffing DTs have limited trade value, but his contract is fair. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Re-signed last year and is a quality starter. Denver's defense relies on its front. Would take a clear overpay to move him, especially with John Franklin-Myers leaving in free agency.

players.name: Malcolm Roach
players.performance_evidence: Extended during November 2025 bye week alongside Wattenberg and Lutz. Versatile interior defender who can play multiple spots. Part of Denver's elite defensive front. Considered a value find from Payton's 'third wave' free agency approach.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid rotational/starter-level DL on a cheap deal ($5.5M cap hit). 3 years of control at 27 makes him a decent value. Worth a mid-4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended in-season. Cheap and productive. With JFM leaving, Roach becomes even more important. Denver would need a compelling offer.
players.injury_notes: Was unavailable through first month of 2025 season.

players.name: Jonathon Cooper
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 10.5 sacks in 2024. Set career bests in sacks, QB hits. Part of best pass-rush duo in NFL with Bonitto (24 combined sacks in 2024 regular season). 4-year, $54M extension (announced as $60M with incentives). 7th-round pick turned elite pass rusher. Started 28 games over last 2 seasons.

trade_value.reasoning: High-end EDGE on a team-friendly $15M AAV deal with 3 years of control. 10.5+ sack upside. Dead cap of $16M complicates a trade, but if moved, he'd command a late 1st/early 2nd from an EDGE-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed extension in November 2024. Dead cap is substantial but not prohibitive in later years. Denver values him as half of their elite EDGE duo. Would take a 1st-round pick equivalent to pry him away.
players.injury_notes: Heart condition (SVT) required ablation procedures pre-draft and early career. Has been medically cleared and healthy since.

players.name: Alex Singleton
players.performance_evidence: 135 tackles in 2025, considered his best season. 4-year Broncos starter and team captain. Played through testicular cancer surgery mid-season (missed only 1 game). LB coach said it's like having DC Vance Joseph in the huddle. Re-signed to 2-year, $15.5M deal with $11M guaranteed.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive tackling LB but age (32) and position value limit trade interest. On a reasonable deal. Worth a 5th-round pick to a LB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed, valued as leader and defensive communicator. But at 32, if Broncos draft an ILB early (they've been linked to first-round ILBs in mocks), Singleton could become available mid-season.
players.injury_notes: Testicular cancer surgery during 2025 season. ACL tear in 2024 (missed time). Remarkable resilience.
players.character_notes: Team captain. Considered one of the strongest leaders in the locker room.

players.name: Justin Strnad
players.performance_evidence: 55% snap share in 2025. Singleton's primary sub and Greenlaw's replacement. Started 8 games in 2024 after Singleton's ACL tear. Re-signed to 3-year, $18M deal — starter money. Developing player who missed his rookie season with wrist injury.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starting LB on a modest deal. 3 years of control at $6M AAV. Worth a late 5th to a team needing LB depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed but Denver is likely to draft an ILB. If a rookie emerges, Strnad's role could diminish and he'd become movable. Fair value would get it done.
players.injury_notes: Missed rookie season (2020) with wrist injury.

players.name: Nik Bonitto
players.performance_evidence: 13.5 sacks in 2024 (3rd in NFL). Second-team All-Pro 2025. Pro Bowl selection. 24 QB hits (T-7th). 16 TFLs. Pick-six vs Colts. Signed 4-year, $106M extension ($120M max with incentives) in September 2025. One half of NFL's best pass-rush duo.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (26) elite EDGE rusher on a 4-year deal with massive guarantees. 13.5 sacks, All-Pro caliber. In theory worth multiple 1sts, but $40M dead cap makes trade impossible. One of the 10-15 most valuable non-QB assets in the NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed $106M extension just 6 months ago. $40M dead cap. 26 years old and ascending. Half of the NFL's premier pass-rush duo. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Payton has expressed concern about snap count/overuse, monitoring his workload.

players.name: Pat Surtain II
players.performance_evidence: Best CB in football. 2024 Defensive Player of the Year. 2022 First-team All-Pro, 2025 Second-team All-Pro. 2025 Pro Bowl. 4-year, $96M extension signed September 2024 — now a bargain with 5 CBs paid more per year. Erases opposing team's top WR. Only missed 3.5 games in 2025 (partially torn pec).

trade_value.reasoning: Best CB in football at age 26 on a now team-friendly deal ($24M AAV with CBs now getting $30M+). 4 years of control. Worth multiple 1sts in theory but $42M dead cap makes trade impossible. Top-5 most valuable defensive player in NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the defense. DPOY winner. $42M dead cap. Contract now looks like a steal as the CB market has exploded past him. Absolute cornerstone — Denver would never trade him.
players.injury_notes: Partially torn pectoral muscle caused him to miss 3.5 games in 2025. Otherwise remarkably durable — missed only 1 game in his first 3 seasons.

players.name: Riley Moss
players.performance_evidence: Earned Proven Performance Escalator (PPE) reflecting strong play. Started all 17 games in each of last 2 seasons. Denver Post praised him as a quality cover man. Beat reporters noted that teams looking for corners could ask about Denver's collection of talent.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting CB2 on a cheap 1-year deal with almost no dead cap. Expiring contract limits value somewhat but the low cost makes him attractive. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a CB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. Broncos drafted Jahdae Barron in the 1st round (2025) and have Kris Abrams-Draine as depth. Denver Post explicitly noted teams could ask about Denver's CB surplus. Moss could be traded for draft capital, especially if Barron is ready to start outside.
players.injury_notes: Minor ankle issues in 2025 but didn't miss games.
players.trade_buzz: Denver Post's roster breakdown noted teams looking for corners could ask about Denver's collection of talent. No specific trade reports yet.

players.name: Ja'Quan McMillian
players.performance_evidence: Among the best slot corners in football per Denver Post. Made game-clinching OT interception vs Bills in Divisional Round. Received 2nd-round restricted free agent tender ($5.767M). UDFA success story. Teams would have to surrender a 2nd-round pick to sign him away as an RFA.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite slot CB at 25 on a 1-year RFA tender. The 2nd-round tender provides protection. Worth a 2nd-round pick — and that's essentially the price to pry him away. Denver would want a 2nd plus a player, or move him for a late 1st in the right deal.

players.availability_reasoning: RFA tender provides protection, but Denver Post noted McMillian will need a major long-term extension soon. If Broncos can't agree on terms, they could flip him for the 2nd-round pick compensation. But they clearly value him — they tendered him at the 2nd-round level.
players.trade_buzz: No active trade buzz, but Denver Post noted the CB room has surplus talent that could draw calls.

players.name: Talanoa Hufanga
players.performance_evidence: First-team All-Pro in 2022 (97 tackles, 4 INTs, 2 sacks, 2 FF). Had healthy 2025 season with Broncos after injury-plagued 2023-24 in SF. Instinctive, athletic safety who excels near LOS. Signed 3-year, $39M deal ($45M max with incentives) in March 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, All-Pro caliber safety at 26. 2 years of control at $13M AAV. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd. Injury history creates some discount.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed just a year ago. Playing well when healthy. Broncos invested heavily in him. Restructure candidate per SI. Would take a clear overpay to move him.
players.injury_notes: Significant injury history: torn ACL (2023), injuries limited him to 7 games in 2024. Played healthy in 2025 with Denver. Health is the key variable.

players.name: Brandon Jones
players.performance_evidence: Second-team All-Pro in 2024. Quality starting FS paired with Hufanga. When both are healthy, they form one of the better safety duos. Entering final year of his deal. Denver Post noted he could be extended or cut to save $7.4M.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting safety on a 1-year deal at $9.2M. Expiring contract limits value. Low dead cap ($1.8M) makes him easily movable. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: No guaranteed money left. Entering final year. Denver Post noted he's a renegotiation candidate — his contract could be dropped substantially with an extension, or he could be cut for $7.4M in savings. If a team offers a mid-round pick, Denver could move him.
players.injury_notes: Foot injury in 2025 but didn't miss significant time.
players.trade_buzz: Listed as renegotiation candidate by SI's cap tracker. Cut candidate per OTC model (75.4% negative fate probability).
